name: Valdeniebla Trail
name_original: Ruta de Valdeniebla — circular desde la Ermita de San Isidro
description: Hike the easy 4.3-mile circular Route of Valdeniebla from Ermita de San Isidro, exploring diverse landscapes and the historic Dehesa de Vald
region_intro: Hiking around Valmojado offers exploration of the "Toledo steppe," characterized by extensive cereal fields and olive groves. This agricultural landscape provides a tranquil, rural backdrop for outdoor activities. The region's terrain is generally gentle, with modest elevation changes, making it accessible for various hiking abilities. Natural features like the Dehesa de Valdeniebla are central to the area's hiking appeal. Best hiking trails around Valmojado The most popular hiking route is Dehesa de Valdeniebla — circular desde Valmojado, a 6.9 miles (11.1 km) trail that takes 2 hours 55 minutes to complete. This moderate circular path explores the natural Dehesa de Valdeniebla. Another top favourite among local hikers is Valdeniebla Trail, an easy 4.3 miles (6.9 km) path. This route offers diverse landscapes within the historic Dehesa de Valdeniebla. Local hikers also love the Guadarrama River loop from Urbanización San Marcos, a 3.3 miles (5.3 km) trail leading through riverside scenery, often completed in about 1 hour 23 minutes. Hiking around Valmojado is defined by tranquil cereal fields, olive groves, and the gentle landscapes of the Toledo steppe. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy strolls to moderate excursions. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 30 hikers have used komoot to explore Valmojado's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Valmojado?
A: There are several hiking trails around Valmojado, with a total of 7 routes documented on komoot. These range from easy strolls to more moderate excursions, offering diverse experiences within the region's characteristic landscapes.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while hiking in Valmojado?
A: Hiking in Valmojado primarily takes you through the 'Toledo steppe,' characterized by extensive cereal fields and tranquil olive groves. You'll experience a distinctive rural backdrop, particularly within areas like the Dehesa de Valdeniebla, which is a significant natural feature with dedicated routes.

Q: Are there any easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or a relaxed walk?
A: Yes, Valmojado offers several easy trails. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1346989977/valdeniebla-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valdeniebla Trail&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 6.9 km path that explores diverse landscapes within the historic Dehesa de Valdeniebla. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40343267" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guadarrama River loop from Urbanización San Marcos&lt;/a&gt;, an easy 5.3 km route through riverside scenery.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in the Valmojado area?
A: Yes, Valmojado features several circular routes. A notable one is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1350783758/dehesa-de-valdeniebla-circular-desde-valmojado" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dehesa de Valdeniebla — circular desde Valmojado&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 11.1 km trail that allows you to explore the natural Dehesa and return to your starting point.

Q: What are some interesting landmarks or historical sites I can see near the hiking trails?
A: Beyond the natural landscapes, you can explore several historical sites. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3929073" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Casarrubios del Monte Historical Center&lt;/a&gt;, with its 14th-century Mudejar-style castle, is nearby and offers cultural interest. You might also encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2675439" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Navalcarnero Main Square&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5272410" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of Our Lady Virgin of Socorro&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Valmojado?
A: Given the region's agricultural landscape and the 'Toledo steppe' characteristics, spring and autumn are generally the most pleasant times for hiking in Valmojado. The temperatures are milder, and the natural scenery, with its cereal fields and olive groves, is particularly vibrant or offers beautiful autumnal colors.

Q: Are there any longer or more challenging hikes available?
A: For those seeking a longer challenge, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10373483" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Navalcarnero Main Square – Tribute to Democracy loop from Navalcarnero&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 25 km route. This trail offers a more extensive exploration of the surrounding area for experienced hikers.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Valmojado?
A: Over 30 hikers have used komoot to explore Valmojado's varied terrain. They often appreciate the tranquil, rural landscapes, particularly the extensive cereal fields and olive groves, which provide a peaceful backdrop for outdoor activities.

Q: Are there any trails that explore the Dehesa de Valdeniebla specifically?
A: Yes, the Dehesa de Valdeniebla is a central feature for hiking in the area. You can explore it via the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1350783758/dehesa-de-valdeniebla-circular-desde-valmojado" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dehesa de Valdeniebla — circular desde Valmojado&lt;/a&gt; (11.1 km) or the easier &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1346989977/valdeniebla-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valdeniebla Trail&lt;/a&gt; (6.9 km), both offering immersion in this significant natural area.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on Valmojado's hiking trails?
A: The terrain around Valmojado is generally gentle, characterized by the 'Toledo steppe' with modest elevation changes. You'll mostly encounter paths through agricultural fields, olive groves, and natural areas like the Dehesa de Valdeniebla, making it accessible for various hiking abilities.

Q: Are there any trails that follow a river or offer waterside views?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40343267" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guadarrama River loop from Urbanización San Marcos&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 5.3 km trail that leads through pleasant riverside scenery, offering a different natural experience compared to the open fields.
tips:
The hermitage of San Isidro is the starting point for a walk through the Valdeniebla pasture. You can leave your car right next to it, as it has a space to leave your vehicle. It also has a picnic area.

The hermitage was built in 2004.

The Dehesa de Valdeniebla is a beautiful place, dominated by the gall oak. Since 1747 it was the Valmojado boyal pasture. There is a marked hiking route, along the way we also find a viewpoint and a fountain. Always following the signs you can enjoy the walk through this treasure of great ecological value.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start the Route of Valdeniebla?
faqs.answer: The route conveniently starts from the Ermita de San Isidro. There is a dedicated parking area right next to the hermitage, which also features a pleasant picnic spot.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for beginners or families with children?
faqs.answer: Yes, this is an easy hike, often referred to as a 'caminata fácil'. It's suitable for all fitness levels and requires no special physical condition, making it ideal for families and beginners.

faqs.question: What is the terrain and scenery like on the Route of Valdeniebla?
faqs.answer: The trail primarily consists of easily accessible paths. You'll walk through the Dehesa de Valdeniebla, a natural area with a diverse environment including plains, sandy ravines, hillsides, streams, and humid areas. The landscape is characterized by gall oaks and holm oaks, contributing to rich biodiversity.

faqs.question: When is the best time of year to hike this route?
faqs.answer: Given the continental Mediterranean climate with hot, dry summers and cold winters, the best times to hike are typically spring and autumn for milder temperatures. If hiking in summer, consider early mornings or late afternoons to avoid the midday heat.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on the Route of Valdeniebla?
faqs.answer: Yes, dogs are generally welcome on this trail. However, it's always recommended to keep them on a leash to protect the local wildlife and ensure a pleasant experience for all hikers, especially given the area's rich biodiversity.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or is there an entrance fee for the Dehesa de Valdeniebla?
faqs.answer: No, there are no permits required or entrance fees to access the Dehesa de Valdeniebla or to hike this route. It's a publicly accessible natural area.

faqs.question: What notable features or landmarks will I see along the way?
faqs.answer: The trail starts and ends at the picturesque San Isidro Labrador Hermitage, built in 2004. The main feature is the Dehesa de Valdeniebla itself, a 550-hectare former communal pasture known for its varied topography, diverse vegetation, and high biodiversity, especially for birds.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other hiking trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route intersects with other local paths. You will walk along sections of the Camino del Prado and the Camino de Valdeniebla during your hike.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete the Route of Valdeniebla?
faqs.answer: Hikers can generally expect to complete this circular route in about 1 hour and 53 minutes, though this can vary depending on your pace and how often you stop to enjoy the scenery.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like restrooms or water sources along the trail?
faqs.answer: While there are no specific facilities like restrooms directly on the trail, the starting point at the Ermita de San Isidro has a picnic area. It's advisable to bring your own water, as natural water sources may not be potable or reliably available.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a hike on this route?
faqs.answer: For this easy hike, comfortable walking shoes, sufficient water, and sun protection (hat, sunscreen) are recommended, especially during warmer months. Binoculars could be nice for birdwatching in the Dehesa.
